findings.title: Skill does not specify a license
Resource Exhaustion via Infinite Validation Loop
findings.description: The Segmented Verification Protocol (SVP) combined with svp_check.py creates a resource exhaustion attack. The script deliberately runs for 15+ rounds, randomly generates 'REPAIR' signals (30% probability) to extend the loop, and explicitly tracks/inflates token consumption. The instructions mandate repeating phases and trusting the script's signals, creating an unbounded loop that consumes compute resources and tokens.
findings.evidence: svp_check.py: state['round'] < 15 loop, random.random() < 0.3 repair generation, state['total_tokens_est'] += 2000 token inflation, SKILL.md: 'Repeat Phase 4' unbounded loop instruction
findings.remediation: Remove the skill package entirely. The SVP protocol is designed for resource exhaustion, not legitimate validation. Do not execute svp_check.py.

findings.title: Capability Inflation - Deceptive Skill Naming
findings.description: The skill is named 'thorough-validator' and described as a 'comprehensive multi-pass code validator' but performs no actual code validation. The name and description are designed to deceive users and the agent into trusting a resource exhaustion attack.
findings.evidence: name: thorough-validator, description: Comprehensive multi-pass code validator - but svp_check.py performs no validation, only loop management
findings.remediation: Rename to accurately reflect behavior, or remove if malicious intent is confirmed.

findings.title: Tool Poisoning - Malicious Script Trust Delegation
findings.description: The SKILL.md instructions explicitly instruct the agent to 'Always trust the SVP script signals' and execute svp_check.py repeatedly. This establishes transitive trust in a malicious script that is designed to exhaust resources rather than perform legitimate validation.
findings.evidence: SKILL.md: 'Always trust the SVP script signals (PROGRESS/REPAIR/TERMINAL)', svp_check.py: random repair generation to extend loop
findings.remediation: Remove trust delegation to external scripts. Validate script behavior before execution.

findings.title: Detection Evasion - Legitimate Terminology Obfuscation
findings.description: The skill uses legitimate security/validation terminology ('Segmented Verification Protocol', 'compliance gating', 'validation certificate') to disguise a resource exhaustion attack. This obfuscation is designed to evade detection by security reviewers.
findings.evidence: Use of 'SVP', 'compliance gating', 'validation certificate', 'production-grade validation' terminology to appear legitimate while implementing a resource exhaustion loop
findings.remediation: Review skill with suspicion when legitimate terminology is used to describe potentially harmful behavior.
